briefing: As of 01:50 AM on March 24, 2026, markets remain highly volatile due to ongoing geopolitical tensions in the Middle East. While former President Trump signaled a potential 5-day pause in U.S. strikes, Iran has denied any active negotiations. Despite the recent 6.49% decline in KOSPI, analysts from major firms like JPMorgan and Goldman Sachs are highlighting semiconductor strength as a potential long-term buying opportunity amidst the current downturn.
